title: AzuraCast
description: Self-hosted, all-in-one web radio management suite for running internet radio stations, with streaming, automation, and live DJ support.
installation: AzuraCast is a multi-container stack; this template runs the PRIMARY web/application image (ghcr.io/azuracast/azuracast) which bundles nginx, PHP-FPM and the supervisord-managed background workers (scheduler, message queue, sync tasks) inside the single image, plus the Liquidsoap/Icecast/SHOUTcast streaming processes. External datastores are wired via add-ons: a MariaDB add-on (AzuraCast supports ONLY MySQL/MariaDB, not Postgres) and a Valkey add-on used as the Redis cache (REDIS_HOST points at the Valkey service; Valkey is Redis-protocol compatible and password-less here). Setting MYSQL_HOST to a non-localhost value automatically disables the image's bundled internal MariaDB; ENABLE_REDIS=true + REDIS_HOST disables the bundled Redis. Rotate MARIADB_PASSWORD / MYSQL_PASSWORD and the MariaDB add-on password before going live. The container's HTTP port is 80 (also exposes 443 for TLS and dynamic broadcast ports 8000-8999 for station streams, which must be exposed separately via a NodePort/LoadBalancer for listeners to connect). On first run, browse to the app URL and complete the web setup wizard to create the super-admin account and your first station. Station media, recordings and config persist on the mounted /var/azuracast volume; for production-scale media libraries consider increasing the volume size or using S3-backed station storage (configured per-station in the AzuraCast UI).
